SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT FOR RETALIATORY EVICTION (TRANSACTION ID # 59204364) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROJO, RICHARD O'HARA, ELIZABETH

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT RICHARD ROJO, ELIZABETH O'HARA NOTICE AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT: FOR RETALIATORY EVICTION IS DENIED. RE: FIRST PRONG OF CCP 425.16 - THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. RE: SECOND PRONG OF CCP 425.16 - OPPOSING PARTY CARRIED ITS BURDEN AND MADE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING FOR RETALIATION CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER CC 1942.5(C), I.E. (1) THAT DEFENDANT LAWFULLY AND PEACEABLY EXERCISED HER RIGHT BY FILING A PERMIT APPEAL; AND (2) THAT LANDLORD ISSUED A "3 DAY NOTICE" AND FILED THE UD BECAUSE TENANT EXERCISED HER RIGHTS. "' SINCE PARTIES RARELY ADMIT AN IMPROPER MOTIVE, MALICE IS USUALLY PROVEN BY CIRCUMSTANTIAL EVIDENCE AND INFERENCES DRAWN FROM THE EVIDENCE." HMS CAPITAL INC. V. LAYERS TITLE COMPANY (2004) 118 CAL.APP.4TH 204. MOVING PARTY CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR ITS PROPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF, IN ORDER TO SATISFY THE SECOND PRONG OF CCP 425.16, ALSO HAS TO DISPROVE THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AVAILABLE TO THE MOVING PARTY UNDER CC 1942.5(E). EVEN IF IT WERE THE CASE, THE COURT WOULD BE REQUIRED TO MAKE A DETERMINATION THAT AS A MATTER OF LAW MOVING PARTY'S ACTIONS WERE BOTH LAWFUL AND IN GOOD FAITH. IN LIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED AND THE INFERENCES FROM SUCH EVIDENCE, THE COURT CAN NOT MAKE SUCH A DETERMINATION AS A MATTER OF LAW. ON AN ANTI-SLAPP THE COURT CANNOT WEIGH THE EVIDENCE. LAFAYETTE MOREHOUSE, INC. V. CHRONICLE PUBL'G CO. (1995) 37 CAL. APP. 4TH 855, 867. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Set For Trial On May-22-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT RICHARD ROJO, ELIZABETH O'HARA Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Off Calendar at the request of the moving party. Case settled. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Set For Trial On Feb-27-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Off Calendar

Notice And Special Motion To Strike Sixth Cause Of Action In Plaintiffs Complaint: For Retaliatory Eviction
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT RICHARD ROJO, ELIZABETH O'HARA Notice And Special Motion To Strike Sixth Cause Of Action In Plaintiffs Complaint: For Retaliatory Eviction IS DENIED. Re: First Prong of CCP 425.16 - The sixth cause of action arises out of protected activity. Re: Second prong of CCP 425.16 - Opposing party carried its burden and made a prima facie showing for retaliation cause of action under CC 1942.5(c), i.e. (1) that Defendant lawfully and peaceably exercised her right by filing a permit appeal; and (2) that landlord issued a "3 Day Notice" and filed the UD because tenant exercised her rights. "' since parties rarely admit an improper motive, malice is usually proven by circumstantial evidence and inferences drawn from the evidence." HMS Capital Inc. v. Layers Title Company (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 204.
Moving party cites no authority for its proposition that Plaintiff, in order to satisfy the second prong of CCP 425.16, also has to disprove the affirmative defense available to the moving party under CC 1942.5(e). Even if it were the case, the Court would be required to make a determination that as a matter of law moving party's actions were both lawful and in good faith. In light of the evidence presented and the inferences from such evidence, the Court can not make such a determination as a matter of law. On an anti-SLAPP the Court cannot weigh the evidence. Lafayette Morehouse, Inc. v. Chronicle Publ'g Co. (1995) 37 Cal. App. 4th 855, 867. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
